---
title: TestClean
date: '2024'
description: NA
categories:
  - Python
format:
  html: default
  ipynb:
    jupyter:
      kernelspec:
        display_name: Python
        language: python3
        name: python3
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    text_representation:
      extension: .qmd
      format_name: quarto
      format_version: '1.0'
      jupytext_version: 1.16.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: Python 3 (ipykernel)
    language: Python
    name: python3
---

FOR REAL DATA MANY FILES
```{python}
#Main block of code for our real files 
import pandas as pd
import os

# Input Folder Path
folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_non_cleaned'

# Collumn variables we want to keep
desired_columns = ['ACK_ID','WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT', 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT', 'SF_SPONS_US_STATE', 'INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT']

# for loop for all .csv's in folder
for filename in os.listdir(folder_path):
    if filename.endswith('.csv'):
        file_path = os.path.join(folder_path, filename)
        
        df = pd.read_csv(file_path)
        
        print(f"Processing file: {filename}")
        print("Original Data:")
        print(df.head(4))
        print("\n")
        
        # Filter by column - get data under the desired column
        filtered_df = df[desired_columns]
        
        print(f"Filtered Data (only {desired_column} column):")
        print(filtered_df.head(4))
        print("\n")
        
        # file output path + file name
        output_file_name = os.path.splitext(filename)[0] + '_cleaned.csv'
        output_file_path = os.path.join(folder_path + '/clean', output_file_name) 
        
        # make a new csv file
        filtered_df.to_csv(output_file_path, index=False)
        
        print(f"Cleaned data saved to: {output_file_path}")
        print("-----------------------------\n")
```


FOR REAL FILES BUT MISSING ACK_ID
Info on state from most likely in C, and info on some sort of plan date
```{python}
#For all real files
import pandas as pd
import os

#input/output folder paths
input_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_non_cleaned'
output_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_cleaned'

# Columns we want
desired_columns = ['ACK_ID','WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT', 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT', 'INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT']

#1999-2008 FILING_ID
#2008-2023 ACK_ID

#MISSING INFO ON STATE LOOK ON FORM C, INS_BROKER_US_STATE MIGHT WORK BUT DOUBT

# Create output folder if it doesn't exist
os.makedirs(output_folder_path, exist_ok=True)

# Process all .csv files in the input folder
for filename in os.listdir(input_folder_path):
    if filename.endswith('.csv'):
        input_file_path = os.path.join(input_folder_path, filename)
        
        try:
            df = pd.read_csv(input_file_path)
            
            print(f"Processing file: {filename}")
            print("Original Data:")
            print(df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Filter by desired columns
            filtered_df = df[desired_columns]
            
            print(f"Filtered Data (only desired columns):")
            print(filtered_df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Output file name and path
            output_file_name = os.path.splitext(filename)[0] + '_cleaned.csv'
            output_file_path = os.path.join(output_folder_path, output_file_name)
            
            # Save filtered data to new CSV file
            filtered_df.to_csv(output_file_path, index=False)
            
            print(f"Cleaned data saved to: {output_file_path}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing {filename}: {str(e)}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")
```

TRYING FOR BOTH FILING AND ACK_ID
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os

# Input/output folder paths
input_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_non_cleaned'
output_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_cleaned'

# Columns we want
desired_columns = ['FILING_ID', 'ACK_ID', 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT', 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT', 'INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT']

# Create output folder if it doesn't exist
os.makedirs(output_folder_path, exist_ok=True)

# Process all .csv files in the input folder
for filename in os.listdir(input_folder_path):
    if filename.endswith('.csv'):
        input_file_path = os.path.join(input_folder_path, filename)
        
        try:
            df = pd.read_csv(input_file_path)
            
            print(f"Processing file: {filename}")
            print("Original Data:")
            print(df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Filter by desired columns, keeping only the columns that exist in the DataFrame
            filtered_df = df[df.columns.intersection(desired_columns)]
            
            # If neither FILING_ID nor ACK_ID is present, create a new column
            if 'FILING_ID' not in filtered_df.columns and 'ACK_ID' not in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df['ID'] = None
            # If FILING_ID is present but ACK_ID is not, rename FILING_ID to ID
            elif 'FILING_ID' in filtered_df.columns and 'ACK_ID' not in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns={'FILING_ID': 'ID'})
            # If ACK_ID is present but FILING_ID is not, rename ACK_ID to ID
            elif 'ACK_ID' in filtered_df.columns and 'FILING_ID' not in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns={'ACK_ID': 'ID'})
            # If both are present, keep them as is
            
            print(f"Filtered Data (only desired columns):")
            print(filtered_df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Output file name and path
            output_file_name = os.path.splitext(filename)[0] + '_cleaned.csv'
            output_file_path = os.path.join(output_folder_path, output_file_name)
            
            # Save filtered data to new CSV file
            filtered_df.to_csv(output_file_path, index=False)
            
            print(f"Cleaned data saved to: {output_file_path}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing {filename}: {str(e)}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")

```



Filter for form 5500 to find state info (geographic) 2009-2023
```{python}
#SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE
#ADMIN_US_STATE
#ID

import pandas as pd
import os
from os import path

input_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_non_cleaned'
output_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_cleaned'

desired_columns = ['FILING_ID', 'ACK_ID', 'ADMIN_US_STATE', 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE']

# Create output folder if it doesn't exist
os.makedirs(output_folder_path, exist_ok=True)

# Process all .csv files in the input folder
for filename in os.listdir(input_folder_path):
    if filename.endswith('.csv'):
        input_file_path = path.join(input_folder_path, filename)
        
        try:
            df = pd.read_csv(input_file_path)
            
            print(f"Processing file: {filename}")
            print("Original Data:")
            print(df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Filter by desired columns, keeping only the columns that exist in the DataFrame
            filtered_df = df[df.columns.intersection(desired_columns)]
            
            # Handle ID column creation
            if 'FILING_ID' in filtered_df.columns and 'ACK_ID' in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df['ID'] = filtered_df['FILING_ID'].fillna(filtered_df['ACK_ID'])
                filtered_df = filtered_df.drop(['FILING_ID', 'ACK_ID'], axis=1)
            elif 'FILING_ID' in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns={'FILING_ID': 'ID'})
            elif 'ACK_ID' in filtered_df.columns:
                filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns={'ACK_ID': 'ID'})
            else:
                filtered_df['ID'] = None
            
            print(f"Filtered Data (only desired columns):")
            print(filtered_df.head(4))
            print("\n")
            
            # Output file name and path
            output_file_name = path.splitext(filename)[0] + '_cleaned.csv'
            output_file_path = path.join(output_folder_path, output_file_name)
            
            # Save filtered data to new CSV file
            filtered_df.to_csv(output_file_path, index=False)
            
            print(f"Cleaned data saved to: {output_file_path}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing {filename}: {str(e)}")
            print("-----------------------------\n")

```


Filter for 1999-2008 to then add to real file

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os
from os import path
import re

input_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_non_cleaned'
output_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_cleaned_1999_2008'

desired_columns = ['FILING_ID', 'SPONS_DFE_STATE', 'ADMIN_STATE']

# Create output folder if it doesn't exist
os.makedirs(output_folder_path, exist_ok=True)

# Process all .csv files in the input folder
for filename in os.listdir(input_folder_path):
    if filename.endswith('.csv'):
        # Extract year from filename
        match = re.search(r'f_5500_(\d{4})', filename)
        if match:
            year = int(match.group(1))
            # Skip files from 2009-2023
            if year >= 2009:
                print(f"Skipping file {filename} as it's from 2009 or later.")
                continue
            
            input_file_path = path.join(input_folder_path, filename)
            
            try:
                df = pd.read_csv(input_file_path)
                
                print(f"Processing file: {filename}")
                print("Original Data:")
                print(df.head(4))
                print("\n")
                
                # Filter by desired columns, keeping only the columns that exist in the DataFrame
                filtered_df = df[df.columns.intersection(desired_columns)]
                
                # Rename FILING_ID to ID
                if 'FILING_ID' in filtered_df.columns:
                    filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns={'FILING_ID': 'ID'})
                else:
                    filtered_df['ID'] = None
                
                # Rename other columns
                column_mapping = {
                    'SPONS_DFE_STATE': 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE',
                    'ADMIN_STATE': 'ADMIN_US_STATE'
                }
                filtered_df = filtered_df.rename(columns=column_mapping)
                
                print(f"Filtered Data (only desired columns):")
                print(filtered_df.head(4))
                print("\n")
                
                # Output file name and path
                output_file_name = path.splitext(filename)[0] + '_cleaned.csv'
                output_file_path = path.join(output_folder_path, output_file_name)
                
                # Save filtered data to new CSV file
                filtered_df.to_csv(output_file_path, index=False)
                
                print(f"Cleaned data saved to: {output_file_path}")
                print("-----------------------------\n")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Error processing {filename}: {str(e)}")
                print("-----------------------------\n")
        else:
            print(f"Skipping file {filename} as it doesn't match the expected format.")
```
































Seperate sheets for each year
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os
import re

input_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_cleaned'
output_folder_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_clean_one_file'

# Function to extract year from filename
def extract_year(filename):
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', filename)
    return int(match.group(1)) if match else None

# Approach 2: Create separate sheets for each year
def create_separate_sheets():
    with pd.ExcelWriter(os.path.join(output_folder_path, 'data_by_year_2.xlsx')) as writer:
        for filename in os.listdir(input_folder_path):
            if filename.endswith('_cleaned.csv'):
                file_path = os.path.join(input_folder_path, filename)
                df = pd.read_csv(file_path)
                year = extract_year(filename)
                sheet_name = f'Year_{year}'
                df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False)
    
    print(f"Data saved to separate sheets in: {os.path.join(output_folder_path, 'data_by_year.xlsx')}")

#run it
create_separate_sheets()

```

Combine info from 5500 dates 2009-2023 and my .xlsx file
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os

# Path to the main Excel file
excel_path = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_clean_one_file/490_data_main.xlsx"

# Path to the folder containing CSV files
csv_folder = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_cleaned"

# Read the Excel file
excel_data = pd.read_excel(excel_path, sheet_name=None)

# Function to determine the CSV filename based on the year
def get_csv_filename(year):
    # Extract the year from the string (assuming format is 'Year_YYYY')
    year_num = int(year.split('_')[-1])
    if year_num < 2009:
        return f"f_5500_{year_num}_cleaned.csv"
    else:
        return f"f_5500_{year_num}_latest_cleaned.csv"

# Process each sheet and merge with corresponding CSV data
for year, df in excel_data.items():
    try:
        csv_filename = get_csv_filename(year)
        csv_path = os.path.join(csv_folder, csv_filename)
        
        if os.path.exists(csv_path):
            csv_data = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
            
            # Check for required columns
            for col in ['SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE', 'ADMIN_US_STATE']:
                if col not in csv_data.columns:
                    csv_data[col] = None  # Add column with None values if it doesn't exist
            
            # Select only the required columns from CSV data
            csv_data = csv_data[['ID', 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE', 'ADMIN_US_STATE']]
            
            # Merge with the Excel data
            merged_data = pd.merge(df, csv_data, on='ID', how='left')
            
            # Update the excel_data dictionary with the merged result
            excel_data[year] = merged_data
        else:
            print(f"CSV file for year {year} not found: {csv_filename}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error processing year {year}: {str(e)}")

# Create a new Excel writer object
output_path = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_clean_one_file/combined_data.xlsx"
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    # Write each dataframe to a separate sheet
    for year, df in excel_data.items():
        df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=str(year), index=False)

print(f"Combined data saved to: {output_path}")
```

Combine info from 5500 dates 1999-2008 and my .xlsx file

```{python}

import pandas as pd
import os

# Path to the main Excel file
excel_path = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_clean_one_file/490_data_main.xlsx"

# Path to the folder containing CSV files for 1999-2008
csv_folder = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/5500_cleaned_1999_2008"

# Read the Excel file
excel_data = pd.read_excel(excel_path, sheet_name=None)

# Function to determine the CSV filename based on the year
def get_csv_filename(year):
    year_num = int(year.split('_')[-1])
    return f"f_5500_{year_num}_cleaned.csv"

# Process each sheet and merge with corresponding CSV data for years 1999-2008
for year, df in excel_data.items():
    year_num = int(year.split('_')[-1])
    if 1999 <= year_num <= 2008:
        try:
            csv_filename = get_csv_filename(year)
            csv_path = os.path.join(csv_folder, csv_filename)
            
            if os.path.exists(csv_path):
                csv_data = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
                
                # Check for required columns
                for col in ['SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE', 'ADMIN_US_STATE']:
                    if col not in csv_data.columns:
                        csv_data[col] = None  # Add column with None values if it doesn't exist
                
                # Select only the required columns from CSV data
                csv_data = csv_data[['ID', 'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE', 'ADMIN_US_STATE']]
                
                # Rename columns to match the desired format
                csv_data = csv_data.rename(columns={
                    'SPONS_DFE_MAIL_US_STATE': 'SPONS_DFE_STATE',
                    'ADMIN_US_STATE': 'ADMIN_STATE'
                })
                
                # Merge with the Excel data
                merged_data = pd.merge(df, csv_data, on='ID', how='left')
                
                # Update the excel_data dictionary with the merged result
                excel_data[year] = merged_data
            else:
                print(f"CSV file for year {year} not found: {csv_filename}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing year {year}: {str(e)}")
    else:
        print(f"Skipping year {year} as it's outside the 1999-2008 range")

# Create a new Excel writer object
output_path = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/real_data_clean_one_file/combined_data.xlsx"
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    # Write each dataframe to a separate sheet
    for year, df in excel_data.items():
        df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=str(year), index=False)

print(f"Combined data saved to: {output_path}")

```

Combine the .xlsx from 1999-2008, 2009-2023
```{python}

import pandas as pd
import os

# Define file paths
input_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/temporary_attempt/'
output_path = 'C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/temporary_attempt/combined/'

# Ensure the output directory exists
os.makedirs(output_path, exist_ok=True)

# Define input file names
file1 = 'new_combined_data_1999-2008.xlsx'
file2 = 'new_combined_data_2009-2023.xlsx'

# Create a new Excel writer object
with pd.ExcelWriter(os.path.join(output_path, 'combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx')) as writer:
    # Process the first file (1999-2008)
    xlsx1 = pd.ExcelFile(os.path.join(input_path, file1))
    for sheet_name in xlsx1.sheet_names:
        df = pd.read_excel(xlsx1, sheet_name)
        df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False)
    
    # Process the second file (2009-2023)
    xlsx2 = pd.ExcelFile(os.path.join(input_path, file2))
    for sheet_name in xlsx2.sheet_names:
        df = pd.read_excel(xlsx2, sheet_name)
        df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False)

print("Files combined successfully. Output saved as 'combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx' in the combined folder.")

```

















































Create new collumn of premiums divided by plan members 
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os

# Input and output file paths
input_file = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/safe_file/combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx"
output_folder = "C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/temp_files"
output_file = os.path.join(output_folder, "processed_combined_data_1999-2023.xlsx")

# Ensure the output folder exists
os.makedirs(output_folder, exist_ok=True)

# Read the Excel file
xls = pd.ExcelFile(input_file)

# Process each sheet
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_file, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    for sheet_name in xls.sheet_names:
        # Read the sheet
        df = pd.read_excel(xls, sheet_name=sheet_name)
        
        # Convert columns to numeric, handling errors
        numeric_columns = ['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT', 'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT', 'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT']
        for col in numeric_columns:
            df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')
        
        # Calculate new columns
        df['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] = df.apply(
            lambda row: 0 if pd.isna(row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT']) or row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT'] == 0 
            else row['WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT'] / row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT'], 
            axis=1
        )
        
        df['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'] = df.apply(
            lambda row: 0 if pd.isna(row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT']) or row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT'] == 0 
            else row['WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT'] / row['INS_PRSN_COVERED_EOY_CNT'], 
            axis=1
        )
        
        # Reorder columns to put new columns in G and H
        columns = df.columns.tolist()
        new_columns = [
            'WLFR_PREMIUM_RCVD_AMT_PER_CAPITA',
            'WLFR_TOT_CHARGES_PAID_AMT_PER_CAPITA'
        ]
        for col in new_columns:
            if col in columns:
                columns.remove(col)
        new_order = columns[:6] + new_columns + columns[6:]
        df = df.reindex(columns=new_order)
        
        # Write the processed dataframe to the new Excel file
        df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False)

print(f"Processing complete. Output file saved as: {output_file}")
```
